title: Why dont Eva 01 look like Lilith split
display_title: Why dont Eva 01 look like Lilith split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Thu Feb 17, 2011 2:41 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Unit 01 is an Adam clone grown through Lilith's body

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Feb 17, 2011 5:11 pm Then how come Unit 01 looks exactly like Adam, then, and not like a giant blob marshmallow man? Unit 01 can be called Lilith's offspring because it was grown from her; it doesn't have to be a proper and exact clone.

[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Thu Feb 17, 2011 5:55 pm Unit 01 has to awaken first, then it reveals its "true form".

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 18, 2011 3:42 pm Which explains...absolutely nothing, because it still looks like Adam either way, and it doesn't explain why it looks like Adam in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Feb 18, 2011 6:18 pm Remember that the crucified Angel was Adam until episode 24. The same tall, glowy Adam had become the marshmallow. This ended up being retconned, but should show that Unit 01 looking like the glowy Adam instead of the marshmallowy Lilith shouldn't be a problem. The forms of Angels aren't particularly constant. It's quite likely Lilith looked just like Adam before the Katsuragi expedition. And look at that sketch from the Test Type OP of the crucified Adam. Looks pretty Lilithy there!

[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Mon Feb 21, 2011 10:05 am AuraTwilight wrote: Then how come Unit 01 looks exactly like Adam, then, and not like a giant blob marshmallow man?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Feb 21, 2011 4:16 pm That's fine, except that still doesn't give a reason why it looks like ADAM specifically, and it doesn't gain an AT Field until Yui enters it, in which case this logic would dictate a giant Yui-bot.

[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Mon Feb 21, 2011 11:20 pm Ritsuko says in episode 23: Rei III that Eva is "man's attempt at making a man that was like a God himself." She said that Adam was "the God that man found but lost." It could simply be that they made it look like Adam simply because that's what they had to go with originally. It could be that simple. That is my best argument for it. AuraTwilight wrote: it doesn't gain an AT Field until Yui enters it

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Feb 22, 2011 8:41 am Yeah, Yui gets absorbed by Unit 01 and doesn't come back. I believe this explains why Unit 01 can activate and produce an AT field even when there is no power. I think she specifically decided to do that to be able to protect Shinji someday. Technically the same thing could have happened to Asuka's mother. Unit 02 originally was used by Asuka's mother... In episode 22: "Don't be." one of the people talking about Asuka's mother says, "You mean was the mental breakdown of the pilot due to the contact experiment?" This led me to believe that Asuka's mother had an incident like Yui's but not as complete. Some part of Asuka's mother was absorbed, however, because in EOE Asuka clearly discovers the presence of her mother inside the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Wed Feb 23, 2011 2:40 pm Allow me then to reword it. Kawaru does approach Rei and say, "How interesting that we have both taken the form of the Lillum for life on this planet." Perhaps this means it is also the form of Adam and Lillith and therefore any offspring OR clones of such would just naturally take the same form. The key, I think is that Adam and Lillith's "Forbidden Fusion" clearly brings about third impact and thus no part of Adam touches Lillith under any circumstances. (Until, of course, Gendo does so.) This renders mixing or grafting or any form of surrogate-ness impossible. Remember that the goal of the angels is to fuse with Lillith... Then consider that Gendo says in episode 22: "Don't Be.", "We cannot allow Unit 01 to be touched by an angel." This is what leads me to believe that Unit 01 is a straight up empty husk clone of Lillith which absorbs Yui to reach its second form and then reaches its third "God" form when, while piloting it, Shinji's AT feild is destroyed. In EOE Adam is fused with Lillith through Rei absorbing it through Gendo's arm and then merging with Lillith. Then Unit one merges with Rei because it is made of the same material because it is the same being essentially. That's what the whole plan is about, so a human being could be involved directly through being absorbed by Unit 01. It's like "sneeking your buddy in" or "smuggling". The whole point of the plan itself is that we get a say, even if it's only one person, what happens after the end of the world. The only reason Shinji ever had the option of returning is because his mother was absorbed first, thus saving him from having to stay behind for all eternity. That's Yui's job. NE ways this is why I think Unit 01 is a straight clone of Lillith with Yui's soul inside. As for it Unit 01 not looking like a marhmallow... It is a clone but it doesn't have a soul, you can't duplicate a soul, but you can "ghetto rig" it by letting it absorb someone elses soul. This is the stipulation that it is a clone but different from Lillith in that it has Yui's soul within it. Kind of a "same engine, different fuel" kind of thing... Am I way off ya think?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Feb 24, 2011 2:10 pm Allow me then to reword it. Kawaru does approach Rei and say, "How interesting that we have both taken the form of the Lillum for life on this planet." Perhaps this means it is also the form of Adam and Lillith and therefore any offspring OR clones of such would just naturally take the same form.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:29 pm It's a plot hole.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:30 pm I don't see what is the problem as Lilith's form is extremely malleable. There's no reason to think Seeds of Life have set form

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Feb 24, 2011 6:22 pm Xard wrote: I don't see what is the problem as Lilith's form is extremely malleable. There's no reason to think Seeds of Life have set form

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 24, 2011 9:15 pm Gehirn/Nerv designed the Evangelions after Adam. This is clearly explained by Ritsuko in 23: Ritsuko in 23 wrote: Humanity found a god and, in their joy, tried to make it theirs. And so they were punished. That was fifteen years ago. The god they had gone to such lengths to find also vanished completely. But then they tried to revive the god on their own. That god was Adam. And, from Adam, humans were created in her image. Those were the Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 24, 2011 10:10 pm There's nothing to say that Eva-01 resembled Adam the most in full armor : if anything, Eva-03/04 should have been a far closer resemblance due to the lack of a ceratopsian horn/frill arrangement. Apart from changes in head design and armor configuration, Evangelion bodies vary very little. Even the harpies, mechanical wings and hideous heads aside, are the same as the other Evas neck-down! I'll be leaving EGK and REGK out of this, for obvious reasons. Sachi wrote: There's no evidence to suggest that, in their natural state, Adam and Lilith appear different. We never get a clear image of Adam other than that being of light, and Lilith is largely deformed throughout most of the plot due to having a fractured soul and stunted growth by means of the Lance of Longinus.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 24, 2011 11:30 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Reichu came up with this a long, long time ago. ::P The whole "Lilith no bunshin" thing is confusing, since the word "bunshin" doesn't exactly translate into "clone". Yes, Eva-01 was created out of Lilith, but does that mean she's a clone of Lilith, or just an Adam clone using Lilith's flesh for body mass? I think the latter is far more likely.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Feb 25, 2011 12:32 am The evidence for this comes from the Extended OP , which includes scenes that are not actually in the show itself. Take a good look at Adam, and then at Lilith (around 1:45 to 1:47). Even if the image quality is poor, the degrading Lilith looks different from Adam and Eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Feb 25, 2011 12:53 am I was referring to your statement when you said that Unit-01 was born with the DNA of Adam and the flesh of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Feb 25, 2011 7:50 am For what they're worth- http://wiki.evageeks.org/Evangelion_Unit-01 http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Eva-01_and_Lilith The key facts are lined up really nicely. 1. Eva-01 looks just like the other Evangelions, once you leave out the usual head/blood color changes. There's nothing at all to indicate that the Eva is somehow different from the "purely Adam-derived" Evas. 2. Eva-01 has a core, which Lilith utterly lacks. Also note the body form taken by Adam, and all the soul-less Eva. Everything that comes out of Adam is easily recognizable as such, and they all look very different from Lilith. 3. There is no evidence to prove that Lilith originally looked very different from the "completed" form she took after Rei merged with her.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Feb 17, 2011 5:11 pm Then how come Unit 01 looks exactly like Adam, then, and not like a giant blob marshmallow man? Unit 01 can be called Lilith's offspring because it was grown from her;
